title: jet.toml
description: Schema for the `jet.toml` file. Generated from the Rust source of truth in `projects/jet/src/task_runner/config.rs`. Run `jet config schema --write` to regenerate.

alias.description:
Module path alias map: alias_prefix → target path.

Overrides matching entries from `tsconfig.json` `compilerOptions.paths`. Example: `"@/" = "./src/"`.

codegen.description: Code-generation settings. These hints let generators choose output compatible with the frontend stack declared by `jet.toml` and `package.json` instead of hard-coding one framework runtime.

lib.description: Library-build settings (`jet build --lib`). Optional because most projects ship an app, not a publishable library; absent → app-mode build. Surfaced here so `[lib]` is a recognized top-level section under `deny_unknown_fields`. @issue #170

resolve.description:
Module resolution settings.

Controls which `exports` conditions are tried when resolving package entries.  Absent → resolver uses default `["import", "browser", "default"]`.

wasm.description: WASM-build (`jet build --wasm`) settings. Optional because not every project uses canvas/WASM; absent → standard JS bundle path. Surfaced here so `[wasm]` is a recognized top-level section under `deny_unknown_fields` — the strict-validation entry point for `[wasm]` keys remains `wasm_build::config::WasmConfig::load_typed` (Slice 2 of #1233).

port.description: TCP port the dev server listens on (default: 3000).

proxy.description:
HTTP reverse proxy map: path prefix → target URL.

Example: ```toml [dev.proxy] "/api" = "http://localhost:3200" "/mcp" = "http://localhost:3200" ```

out_dir.description:
Output directory for production build (relative to project root).

Example: `out_dir = "../be/static"`.

LibConfig.description:
`[lib]` section of `jet.toml` — `jet build --lib` settings.

Every field is optional so `[lib]` can be present (opting the project into library-build mode) with all behaviour defaulted. Entries default to the `package.json` `exports`/`module`/`main` discovery; formats default to `["esm"]`. @spec .aw/tech-design/projects/jet/semantic/jet-task-runner.md#schema @issue #170

css_merge.description: CSS cascade-merge: an ordered list of `style.css` files (paths relative to the project root, e.g. dependent packages' `dist/style.css`) to concatenate into the output `style.css`. Declared order IS cascade order: files are concatenated in the order given (first listed appears first in the output, so its rules can be overridden by later ones). When `None`, no cascade-merge runs and the lib build is byte-identical to today. Replaces the bespoke `mergeDepStyles` vite plugin.

dts.description: Emit `<entry>.d.ts` type-declaration files alongside the JS output (isolatedDeclarations-style). When `None`, defaults to `true` for library mode. `jet build --lib --dts` / `--no-dts` override this. @issue #171

entry.description: Explicit entry source paths. When `None`, entries are discovered from `package.json` `exports` (falling back to `module`/`main`).

externalize_all.description: Force-externalize all bare package specifiers. Library builds always externalize `dependencies`/`peerDependencies`; this widens that to every bare import. Default `true` for library mode.

formats.description: Output formats to emit, e.g. `["esm", "cjs"]`. When `None`, defaults to `["esm"]`.

global_name.description: Global variable name for `iife` output (`jet build --lib --format iife`), e.g. `MyLib`. When `None`, a name is derived from the `package.json` `name`. `jet build --lib --global-name <name>` overrides this.

out_dir.description: Output directory (relative to project root). When `None`, defaults to the `[build]` `out_dir`, then `dist`.

preserve_modules.description: Preserve the internal module structure (one output per source module) instead of bundling each entry. Default `false`. Supports ESM/CJS output. `jet build --lib --preserve-modules` overrides this.

raw_copy.description: Raw-asset copy: directories to copy verbatim into the output dir, preserving subpaths, so consumers can deep-import `@pkg/assets/icons/x.svg`. Each [`RawCopy`] names a `from` directory (relative to the project root) and an optional `to` destination (relative to the out dir; default = the same relative path as `from`). When `None`, no copy runs. Replaces the bespoke `copyRawAssets` vite plugin.

hooks.description: Hook runtime to emit. `auto` emits React Query hooks only for a React stack that declares `@tanstack/react-query`.

OpenApiCodegenHooks.description:
Hook runtime selector for OpenAPI codegen.

@spec .aw/tech-design/projects/jet/interfaces/cli/openapi-client-codegen-types-fetch-client-react-query-hooks.md#logic

OpenApiCodegenHttpClient.description:
HTTP backend selector for OpenAPI codegen.

@spec .aw/tech-design/projects/jet/interfaces/cli/select-http-client-backend-fetch-axios-for-openapi-codegen.md#logic

OpenApiCodegenStack.description:
Frontend stack selector for OpenAPI codegen.

@spec .aw/tech-design/projects/jet/interfaces/cli/openapi-client-codegen-types-fetch-client-react-query-hooks.md#logic

RawCopy.description:
One raw-asset directory copy directive within `[lib].raw_copy`.

Example: ```toml [[lib.raw_copy]] from = "assets/icons" to = "icons" ``` @spec .aw/tech-design/projects/jet/semantic/jet-task-runner.md#schema

from.description: Source directory to copy verbatim, relative to the project root.

to.description: Destination directory relative to the out dir. When `None`, the tree is copied to the same relative path as `from`.

ResolveConfig.description:
Module resolution configuration block.

Deserialized from the `[resolve]` section of `jet.toml`.

Example: ```toml [resolve] conditions = ["import", "node", "default"] ```

conditions.description:
Ordered export conditions for package.json `exports` resolution.

When `None`, the resolver defaults to `["import", "browser", "default"]`.

RootPropValue.description: Prop value — numbers, booleans, strings, or homogeneous arrays. The generated factory takes `i64` / `bool` / `String` / `Vec<T>`, matching the prop interface's Rust lowering. Display impl emits valid Rust source that can be interpolated directly into the WASM-entry factory call. @spec .aw/tech-design/projects/jet/semantic/jet-wasm-build.md#schema

anyOf.description: Homogeneous array — serializes as `vec![elem, ...]`. Inner elements reuse the `RootPropValue::Display` contract, so nested arrays also work (though the transpiler only lowers one level today).

TaskDef.description: Single task definition within the pipeline. @spec .aw/tech-design/projects/jet/semantic/jet-task-runner.md#schema

cache.description: Whether this task is cacheable (default: true).

command.description:
Shell command to execute for this task.

Used for pipeline hooks such as `pipeline.tailwind`. Example: `command = "tailwindcss -i ./src/index.css -o ./src/output.css --watch"`.

env.description: Environment variables that affect the cache key.

inputs.description: Glob patterns for input files that affect the cache key.

outputs.description: Glob patterns for output files to cache.

persistent.description: Whether this is a long-running task (e.g., dev server). Persistent tasks are never cached.

watch.description: Whether this is a watch-mode task (used by pipeline hooks).

web_server.description: Web-server spawned before the runner starts and killed after it ends.

entry.description: Path to the TSX entry file, relative to the project root.

renderer.description: Renderer backend used by the generated browser entrypoint.

root_component.description: Root component name (PascalCase), must match an exported function component in `entry`.

root_props.description: Props passed to the root component factory. Order matches the TS interface field order.

WebServerConfig.description: `[test.web_server]` section. Spawned as a subprocess during `jet test`; the runner waits for the port (or `url`) to accept connections before dispatching any specs.

command.description: Shell command to spawn. Run under `sh -c` so pipes/env work.

cwd.description: Working directory for `command` (relative to project root). Default is the project root.

port.description: TCP port to poll on `127.0.0.1`. Ignored when `url` is set.

reuse_existing.description: If true and the port (or URL) is already accepting connections, skip spawning and leave the external server alone. Default true.

url.description: HTTP URL to poll with a GET request. Takes precedence over `port`.
